title: Internal AI Developer Tool / Scaffold Generator
description: Build an internal AI engineering tool that helps another builder move faster, with strong UX, instrumentation, and a clear product story.
whyItMatters: Internal developer tooling is one of the fastest ways to signal platform and product maturity at once. It feels close to how frontier teams actually create leverage.
prerequisites: Complete at least 5 of the earlier projects
Comfortable with LLM APIs, deployment, and monitoring

steps.title: Identify a real problem
steps.description: Find a genuine problem that AI can solve better than existing solutions. Talk to potential users. Validate that the problem is real and people would use a solution. Write a one-page product brief.
steps.tips: The best AI products are not general-purpose — they solve a specific problem for a specific audience extremely well.

steps.title: Design the architecture
steps.description: Use the AI System Design Template. Define: data sources, model choices, API structure, storage, caching, and monitoring. Make buy-vs-build decisions for each component. Estimate costs at expected scale.

steps.title: Build the AI backend
steps.description: Implement the core AI functionality: LLM integration, RAG pipeline, custom models, or agent logic — whatever your product requires. Focus on reliability: error handling, fallbacks, and consistent output quality.

steps.title: Build the user interface
steps.description: Create a clean, functional UI. It does not need to be beautiful, but it must be usable. Handle AI-specific UX challenges: loading states, streaming responses, confidence indicators, error messages, and feedback mechanisms.

steps.title: Add monitoring and feedback
steps.description: Instrument everything: API latency, model accuracy, user engagement, error rates, and cost per request. Add a feedback mechanism (thumbs up/down, corrections) so users can tell you when the AI gets it wrong.

steps.title: Deploy and test with real users
steps.description: Deploy to a cloud provider. Share with 5-10 real users (friends, colleagues, or online communities). Collect feedback. Fix the most critical issues.

steps.title: Iterate based on feedback
steps.description: Use feedback and monitoring data to improve: fix common failure modes, improve prompts, add missing features, optimise cost. Ship at least 3 meaningful improvements based on user feedback.

steps.title: Write the case study
steps.description: Document the entire journey: problem statement, architecture decisions, technical challenges, user feedback, metrics, and lessons learned. This becomes the centrepiece of your AI engineering portfolio.

deliverables: Deployed AI-powered product accessible via URL
User feedback summary and iteration log
Monitoring dashboard showing real usage metrics
Case study document covering the full product journey
tools: Cloud platform (Railway, Render, AWS, or GCP)

rubric.description: The product solves a real problem for real users. Evidence of user research and validation.
Clean architecture, reliable AI integration, proper error handling, and monitoring. The system works consistently.
The product is usable without instructions. AI-specific UX challenges are handled well.
Evidence of meaningful improvements based on user feedback and monitoring data. At least 3 shipped improvements.
The case study is well-written and demonstrates end-to-end AI engineering capability. A hiring manager would be impressed.
